text: The opposing side leans on a contested analytical protocol for the toxicology findings. Whether the methodology holds up at the admissibility stage is the hinge on whether we move on the suppression motion at all.

Literature read so far is thin and split. One validation study defends the protocol's reliability; a separate critique flags inadequate blank-sample controls but draws on a small sample and has not been replicated. No consensus error figure is established. The question is unsettled.
Conclusion stays open until the science is solid. Do not state a finding we cannot defend, and do not move on the motion or let anything go out as final while the methodology question is unresolved.
